claim_text: Hormone replacement therapy prevents coronary heart disease in healthy postmenopausal women.
Encainide and flecainide reduce mortality after myocardial infarction by suppressing ventricular arrhythmias.
Peptic ulcers are caused by stress and excess gastric acid, not by bacterial infection.
Hydroxychloroquine reduces mortality in hospitalized COVID-19 patients.
Vitamin E supplementation reduces cardiovascular events.
Rofecoxib (Vioxx) is a cardiovascularly safe anti-inflammatory.
Tight (intensive) glucose control reduces mortality in critically ill patients.
Estrogen plus progestin therapy reduces the risk of dementia in postmenopausal women.
Statins reduce cardiovascular mortality in patients at elevated cardiovascular risk.
ACE inhibitors reduce mortality in patients with heart failure with reduced ejection fraction.
Periconceptional folic acid supplementation reduces neural tube defects.
Aspirin reduces recurrent vascular events in secondary prevention after myocardial infarction.
Eradicating Helicobacter pylori heals peptic ulcers and reduces recurrence.
Antihypertensive therapy reduces stroke risk in patients with hypertension.
Insulin therapy is required to treat type 1 diabetes mellitus.
